data.title: NB Bancorp adopts share buyback plan for up to 5% of shares outstanding
data.description:
Bank holding company NB Bancorp (NASDAQ:NBBK) announced on Wednesday a stock repurchase plan for up to 2.03M shares of common stock, representing ~5% of the shares outstanding.

Shares were +1.11% to $17.36 at noon.

The stocks may be repurchased in open market or private transactions, through block trades, or pursuant to any suitable trading plan.

The repurchase prices are subject to the management's discretion.

data.title: Tripadvisor shares surge after Q1 results beat expectations
data.description:
Tripadvisor (NASDAQ:TRIP) jumped as much as 13% on Wednesday, reaching a five-week high, after the travel platform reported better-than-expected first-quarter earnings and signaled confidence heading into the peak summer travel season.

Revenue for the first quarter rose 1% year-over-year to $398 million, beating the average estimate of $388 million among Wall Street analysts.

Adjusted net income of $21 million, or $0.14 a share, beat the consensus of $0.05 a share. GAAP results showed a net loss of $11 million, or $0.08 a share. 

Adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization came in at $44 million, or 11% of revenue. While the figure was lower than the prior year’s $47 million, Chief Financial Officer Mike Noonan said in a conference call with analysts that the quarter reflected healthy performance across its marketplace and hotel segments, with the company positioned well to meet full-year expectations.

Chief Executive Matt Goldberg said the company is entering the travel season with strong momentum and remains confident in its long-term growth trajectory, even amid macroeconomic uncertainty.

"We see a number of opportunities ahead where we believe we're uniquely positioned to drive meaningful value, including scaling our marketplaces, starting with experiences," he said in a call with analysts.

Segment results

Among business segments, Viator, Tripadvisor’s (NASDAQ:TRIP) experiences platform, continued to be a standout performer. It reported a 10% increase in revenue to $156 million, along with a 15% rise in bookings and narrowed its adjusted ebitda loss from $27 million to $18 million.

TheFork, Tripadvisor’s (TRIP) restaurant booking arm, grew revenue by 12% to $46 million and also trimmed losses. In contrast, the core Tripadvisor brand saw revenue decline 8% to $219 million, reflecting ongoing softness in hotel and advertising revenue.

Balance sheet stability

Despite flat overall revenue growth, investors appeared encouraged by Tripadvisor’s (TRIP) improved net margin, shrinking GAAP loss, strong cash position and continued strength in its Viator and TheFork units. The company ended the quarter with $1.2 billion in cash and equivalents, up $90 million from the end of 2024.

Tripadvisor (TRIP) also completed its previously announced $430 million merger with Liberty TripAdvisor Holdings (LTRIP) in April. As part of that deal, the company forgave a $327 million loan extended to LTRIP, absorbing the amount into the total transaction value. In March, the company also secured a $350 million incremental Term Loan B to help refinance outstanding debt and support general corporate purposes.

Tripadvisor’s (TRIP) stock peaked in 2014 amid optimism that it would dominate the online travel market with its massive user-generated content base and advertising-driven business model. At the time, it was seen as a key player in the digital shift away from traditional travel agencies.

But over the past decade, the company has struggled to keep pace with larger, more vertically integrated competitors such as Booking Holdings (BKNG) (BKNG:CA) and Expedia Group (EXPE), which offer end-to-end travel solutions. Rising competition in digital ads, the shift toward mobile-first platforms, and Tripadvisor’s (TRIP) reliance on a declining metasearch model eroded its market share and margins.

Despite efforts to pivot toward experiences and dining, and to monetize platforms like Viator and TheFork, the company has lost over 80% of its market value since its peak, reflecting investor concerns about its ability to reclaim a leadership role in an increasingly crowded and consolidated travel tech landscape.

data.title: SA analyst upgrades: TSLA, MSFT, AMD, PLTR, VRTX, ONON, CB
data.description:
Here are the latest rating upgrades by Seeking Alpha analysts:

Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA). Hold to buy by Marc Gerstein. "Recognizing the degree to which the Street is choosing to focus on TSLA’s (NASDAQ:TSLA) grand vision, believing the ideas to be credible, and recognizing that investors have already incorporated brand controversies into their TSLA (TSLA) expectations, I now see TSLA (TSLA) as likely outperforming the broader market."

Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT). Sell to buy by Bay Area Ideas. "Fundamentals are robust with Q3 revenue up 13% YoY, strong Azure growth, and optimistic Q4 guidance, justifying current valuation. Despite a high P/S ratio, Microsoft's (NASDAQ:MSFT) leadership in AI cloud infrastructure and solid fundamentals make it a worthy investment."

Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD). Sell to buy by Tech Stock Pros. "We expect the stock to trade up in the near-term and recommend investors add this name to their portfolio but not snooze on it because in about two quarters AMD’s (NASDAQ:AMD) risk-reward profile could shift heavily for the worst once Intel (INTC) completes its 18A node which will be incorporated into Panther Lake, scheduled for release in 2H25 and mass production in 2026."

Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR). Sell to hold by A.J. Button. "Were PLTR’s (NASDAQ:PLTR) EPS to double every year for the next three years, while its price remained the same, it would still be trading at a fairly high P/E ratio. You need a lot of faith in a company to pay that kind of price for its stock."

Vertex Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:VRTX). Hold to buy by ONeil Trader. "Vertex (NASDAQ:VRTX) missed Q1 revenue and EPS estimates, but this is not a cause for concern as the full-year outlook has improved…I am upgrading Vertex (VRTX) from hold to buy based on Journavx early launch metrics and faster pipeline progress."

On (NYSE:ONON). Sell to hold by Caffital Research. "The current growth narrative supports ONON's (NYSE:ONON) expensive valuation. While I estimate upside to a fair value of $57.5, potential changes in the narrative are still quick to change the value."

Chubb (NYSE:CB). Hold to buy by Curonian Research. "Despite a deceleration in growth, Chubb's (NYSE:CB) intrinsic value is 16% higher than its market capitalization, making it an attractive investment at current prices."

data.title: Lantheus Holdings stock drops on lowered full year outlook
data.description:
Lantheus Holdings (NASDAQ:LNTH) shares plunged on Wednesday after it lowered its full year guidance, and reported results below expectations.

The company now expects full year revenue guidance in the range of $1.550B to $1.585B, lower than the previous range of $1.545B to $1.610B.

Adjusted profit for 2025 is expected between $6.60 per share and $6.70 per share, compared to previous range between $7.00 per share and $7.20 per share.

"With the expiry of PYLARIFY pass-through status and the implementation of mean unit cost payment for Medicare FFS and the hospital outpatient setting, we did experience what we believe will be temporal competitive disruption among smaller, non-contracted sites," chief commercial officer Amanda Morgan said in a post-earnings call.

The Massachusetts-based company also posted first quarter revenue of $372.8M, below consensus of $379.14M. Adjusted profit of $1.53 per share also missed analysts' expectation of $1.65 per share.

"We are shifting our implicit PYLARIFY year-over-year range to flat-to-low single-digit percent growth for the full year versus our prior view of low-single-digit to mid-single-digit growth," chief financial officer Bob Marshall said.

The stock of the company is down about 20% today.

data.title: IFF targets $10.6B to $10.9B in 2025 sales amid strategic realignment
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: International Flavors & Fragrances Inc. (IFF) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Erik Fyrwald highlighted IFF's strong start to 2025, achieving notable currency-neutral growth across most business segments, including Taste, Pharma Solutions, Scent, and Health & Biosciences. He emphasized the strategic importance of separating the Nourish segment into Taste and Food Ingredients for better tracking and accountability.

  • Fyrwald confirmed the completion of the Pharma Solutions divestiture ahead of schedule, stating that it strengthens the company's capital structure and achieves the long-term net debt to credit-adjusted EBITDA target of below 3x.

  • CFO Michael DeVeau reported $2.8 billion in Q1 sales, representing 3% currency-neutral growth, with adjusted operating EBITDA of $578 million, a 9% increase on a comparable currency-neutral basis.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed full-year 2025 guidance, setting sales expectations between $10.6 billion and $10.9 billion, indicating 1% to 4% currency-neutral growth, and adjusted operating EBITDA in the range of $2 billion to $2.15 billion.

  • The divestiture of Pharma Solutions is expected to reduce 2025 revenue by approximately 7% and adjusted EBITDA by 8%, while foreign exchange impacts are expected to be less significant than previously forecasted.

  • Management expressed confidence in mitigating tariff impacts through operational adjustments and targeted pricing surcharges, projecting no material deviation in guidance due to these factors.

Financial Results

  • The Taste segment achieved $627 million in sales, a 7% year-over-year increase, driven by broad-based volume growth and favorable pricing.

  • Pharma Solutions delivered $266 million in revenue and $54 million in adjusted operating EBITDA, an increase of 19% year-over-year.

  • Food Ingredients generated $796 million in sales, down 4% year-over-year due to weaker protein solution volumes and purposeful reductions in low-margin pectin sales. However, adjusted operating EBITDA for this segment grew 5% due to pricing and productivity improvements.

  • Health & Biosciences and Scent segments also recorded positive growth, with notable contributions from grain processing and fine fragrances, respectively.

Q&A

  • Nicola Tang, BNP Paribas, inquired about portfolio resilience amid recessionary risks. Fyrwald underscored that 80% of IFF’s portfolio lies in essential products, with discretionary areas like fine fragrances experiencing strong order patterns.

  • Josh Spector, UBS, asked about tariff impacts. DeVeau disclosed a $100 million tariff-related cost exposure for 2025 but emphasized mitigation strategies, including production redistribution and pricing surcharges.

  • Kevin McCarthy, Vertical Research Partners, sought details on IFF’s €130 million joint venture with Kemira. Fyrwald stated that it involves scaling enzymatic biomaterials and constructing a plant in Finland by 2027.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns about macroeconomic uncertainties, including tariff impacts and consumer slowdown. Questions often focused on the sustainability of growth and IFF's ability to maintain guidance. Analyst tone was cautious but less skeptical than the previous quarter.

  • Management maintained a confident tone, particularly when discussing productivity gains and completed divestitures. Fyrwald’s focus on innovation and long-term investments was consistent with prior quarters.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2025 remains unchanged, though foreign exchange impacts and divestiture adjustments have been revised.

  • Management's tone showed consistent confidence, emphasizing resilience and operational discipline despite macroeconomic challenges. Analysts continued to probe tariff and consumer demand concerns, mirroring similar themes from the prior quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Tariff impacts on costs and supply chain disruptions were highlighted. Management is proactively mitigating these through operational adjustments and pricing strategies.

  • Analysts expressed concerns over macroeconomic uncertainties and potential inventory destocking in discretionary categories.

Final Takeaway

IFF's Q1 2025 results showcase strong operational execution and progress in strategic realignment. With the successful divestiture of Pharma Solutions and reaffirmed full-year guidance, the company is positioned to navigate macroeconomic uncertainties while focusing on growth in core segments like Taste, Scent, and Health & Biosciences. Management's attention to innovation and productivity provides a solid foundation for sustainable, long-term profitability.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Shionogi to buy Japan Tobacco’s Torii Pharma in a deal worth over $1B
data.description:
• Japanese pharmaceutical company Shionogi (OTCPK:SGIOY) announced Wednesday an agreement with Japan Tobacco (OTCPK:JAPAF) to acquire its subsidiary Torii Pharmaceutical (OTCPK:TRXPF) in a deal worth ¥160B ($1.1B).
  • Under the deal terms, Osaka-based Shionogi (OTCPK:SGIOF) will buy 54.84% of Japan Tobacco’s (OTCPK:JAPAY) voting rights in Torii (OTCPK:TRXPF) for ¥70.3B before extending a tender offer for the company's minority shareholders at ¥6,350 yen per share.
  • The buyout consideration for minority investors represents a 21% premium to Torii’s (OTCPK:TRXPF) closing price before the deal was announced.
  • In a statement on Wednesday, Torii’s (OTCPK:TRXPF) board of directors endorsed Shionogi’s (OTCPK:SGIOF) offer and urged its investors to tender their shares in support of the deal.
  • While Shionogi (OTCPK:SGIOY) specializes in infectious disease drugs, Tokyo-headquartered Torii (OTCPK:TRXPF) is a leading Japanese drug developer targeting conditions such as chronic kidney disease, skin diseases, and allergies.

data.title: Lake Resources to explore strategic alternatives for Kachi lithium project
data.description:
Australia's Lake Resources (OTCQB:LLKKF) said Wednesday it launched a strategic review of its flagship Kachi lithium project in Argentina, believing the asset is significantly undervalued, with potential options under consideration including a stake sale in the project and a potential sale or merger of the company.

Lake Resources (OTCQB:LLKKF) said the decision to explore strategic alternatives also is bolstered by recent instances of companies with Argentine lithium projects receiving proposals significantly exceeding their current market capitalizations.

The Kachi lithium brine project is the largest independent project across South America's lithium triangle, with total resources exceeding 10.6M metric tons of lithium carbonate equivalent.

Prices of lithium carbonate last month reached their lowest levels since August 2021 alongside prolonged weakness in demand growth from the electric vehicle sector.

data.title: Cadre Holdings outlines $618M-$648M revenue guidance for 2025 amid nuclear market expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Cadre Holdings (CDRE) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Warren Kanders highlighted that Q1 results exceeded expectations, driven by sustained demand for mission-critical safety products and effective strategic execution. The company completed the acquisition of the engineering division from Cars Group, expanding its nuclear safety vertical. He emphasized that the acquisition is a critical step towards scaling nuclear capabilities and unlocking growth opportunities in automation, robotics, and nuclear medicine.

  • Kanders also noted a robust M&A pipeline with potential targets across nuclear, law enforcement, first responder, and military markets. He stated that Cadre is confident in its ability to navigate economic uncertainties, citing the resilience of its business model.

  • CFO Blaine Browers disclosed that Q1 net sales reached $130.1 million, and adjusted EBITDA was $20.5 million. The gross margin improved by 130 basis points year-over-year, attributed to favorable pricing and previous inventory amortization.

  • President Brad Williams elaborated on the acquisition's value, emphasizing entry into international markets and enhanced capabilities in remote handling and robotics. He also highlighted a 9% dividend increase, maintaining the company’s approach to shareholder returns.

Outlook

  • The company provided full-year 2025 guidance, projecting net sales between $618 million and $648 million and adjusted EBITDA between $112 million and $122 million, with margins anticipated at 18.5%.

  • Management expects the newly acquired engineering division to contribute approximately $46 million in net sales and $6.5 million in EBITDA for 2025.

  • Despite tariff concerns, management stated they are implementing mitigation strategies to offset potential impacts fully.

Financial Results

  • Q1 net sales were $130.1 million, with adjusted EBITDA at $20.5 million. Gross margin improved by 130 basis points compared to the prior year.

  • The company reaffirmed organic growth guidance and noted that the acquisition of the Cars engineering division contributed to the positive adjustments in their projections.

  • The balance sheet remains strong, with a pro forma net leverage ratio under 1.75x, allowing for continued investment in growth initiatives.

Q&A

  • Greg Konrad, Jefferies: Questioned pricing impacts and forward-looking contributions. CFO Blaine Browers confirmed that Q1 pricing adjustments were standard and that current expectations include full tariff offsets for the rest of the year.

  • Mark Smith, Lake Street Capital Markets: Asked about timing of shipments and lumpiness in revenues. CFO Browers noted that Q4 projects typically skew results, and the second half of 2025 is expected to be stronger due to project timing.

  • Jeff Van Sinderen, B. Riley Securities: Sought details on nuclear market demand and M&A priorities. President Williams outlined a strong growth outlook for the nuclear sector, supported by international expansion and synergies from recent acquisitions.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, frequently pressing for specifics on tariff impacts and M&A integration strategies. Their tone was neutral to slightly positive, reflecting confidence in Cadre’s operational resilience.

  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout the call, emphasizing the company's ability to navigate uncertainties and leverage its operating model for growth.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Compared to Q4 2024, management's tone remained consistent, with a focus on operational resilience and strategic growth. However, Q1 2025 displayed higher emphasis on M&A impacts and integration details.

  • Financial performance in Q1 was below Q4’s record-breaking results, largely due to seasonal and project-specific factors.

  • Analysts' questions in Q1 were more focused on tariffs and geopolitical uncertainties, a shift from Q4’s emphasis on organic growth and backlog execution.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged macroeconomic uncertainties, including tariff impacts and potential delays in procurement processes within federal agencies.

  • Mitigation strategies include pricing adjustments, productivity enhancements, and diversification of production facilities.

Final Takeaway

Cadre Holdings delivered a strong start to 2025 with results exceeding expectations, bolstered by the acquisition of the Cars engineering division and sustained demand in its core markets. With a robust M&A pipeline and strategic focus on nuclear and law enforcement verticals, the company projects 2025 revenues between $618 million and $648 million. Management’s proactive strategies to mitigate tariff impacts and optimize operational efficiencies reflect confidence in navigating economic uncertainties while maintaining growth momentum.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Jack henry outlines 2025 outlook with 6%-6.5% revenue growth amid cloud migration and M&A activity
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Jack Henry & Associates (JKHY) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Greg Adelson highlighted a solid performance this quarter, with non-GAAP revenue increasing by 7% and operating margin expanding 207 basis points year-over-year to 23%. He emphasized the strength of recurring revenue, which accounted for 92%, and noted significant growth in processing and cloud revenues.
  • Adelson discussed fiscal 2025 guidance adjustments, citing macroeconomic challenges and lower revenue from nonstrategic areas like hardware and consulting projects. Full-year deconversion revenue is now forecasted between $22 million and $28 million.
  • The company secured 28 new core contracts in fiscal 2025, including 11 in Q3, representing $30 billion in assets. Adelson noted a 55% increase in assets under migration to the Jack Henry private cloud compared to the previous year.
  • Adelson shared progress in the payments segment, with significant growth in clients adopting Zelle, RTP, and FedNow. The Banno Digital Platform grew to over 13.7 million registered users, an 18% increase year-over-year.
  • CFO Mimi Carsley reported Q3 GAAP revenue increased by 9%, driven by deconversion revenue. Non-GAAP revenue growth was tempered by lower contributions from hardware sales and project delays.

Outlook

  • Fiscal 2025 guidance includes non-GAAP revenue growth of 6%-6.5%, a 60-70 basis point margin expansion, and GAAP EPS of $6.00 to $6.09. Free cash flow conversion is expected to remain in the range of 65%-75%.
  • Adelson projected continued client migrations to the Jack Henry private cloud, with 76% of clients already transitioned. He also anticipates accelerated competitive core wins in Q4.
  • Carsley noted a cautious stance for the remainder of the fiscal year, with potential impacts from softened consumer-related payments and delayed project implementations.

Financial Results

  • Q3 non-GAAP revenue grew by 7%, while GAAP revenue rose by 9%. Non-GAAP operating margin reached 23%, up 207 basis points compared to the prior year.
  • Deconversion revenue was $9.6 million, contributing to the adjusted annual forecast of $22 million to $28 million.
  • Core revenue growth remained robust, driven by data processing and hosting, while private and public cloud offerings expanded by 11%.
  • Payments segment revenue increased by 7%, supported by growth in card and payment processing products, including FedNow and RTP.
  • Operating cash flow for the quarter was $108 million, with a trailing 12-month free cash flow of $303 million.

Q&A

  • Dan Perlin, RBC: Questioned the delays in hardware purchases and cloud migration. CEO Adelson clarified that delays were mostly in nonrecurring projects, with private cloud migrations progressing as planned.
  • Rayna Kumar, Oppenheimer: Asked about the impact of project delays. Adelson stated delays are pushing implementations into fiscal 2026 but do not indicate cancellations.
  • Jason Kupferberg, Bank of America: Inquired about fiscal 2026 prospects. CFO Carsley cited robust sales pipelines and no elongation of sales cycles, emphasizing confidence in ongoing core wins.
  • Vasu Govil, KBW: Sought clarity on revenue guidance changes. Carsley attributed adjustments to hardware sales declines and delayed client projects.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts exhibited a neutral to slightly cautious tone, focusing on project delays and macroeconomic impacts. Questions reflected concerns about revenue growth sustainability and client spending trends.
  • Management maintained a confident tone both in prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing long-term growth drivers like cloud migration and payments innovation. Adelson repeatedly highlighted the company’s execution capabilities and competitive positioning.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management displayed a consistent sentiment, with management offering reassurances regarding fiscal 2025 guidance adjustments.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue guidance was revised downward from the previous quarter, reflecting macroeconomic challenges and project delays. However, margin and EPS guidance were raised, showcasing effective cost controls.
  • Core wins and cloud migrations accelerated, with notable growth in asset sizes of new clients.
  • Analysts' focus shifted slightly from revenue growth to understanding the impact of macroeconomic factors and project timelines.
  • Management's confidence in achieving long-term goals remained consistent, supported by strong recurring revenue growth and strategic innovation efforts.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited challenges in nonstrategic revenue areas, including hardware purchases and consulting projects, with some delays pushing implementations into fiscal 2026.
  • Analysts raised concerns about potential impacts of ongoing M&A activity and consumer payment trends on revenue growth.
  • Mitigation strategies include focusing on high-margin cloud and processing revenues, along with disciplined cost management.

Final Takeaway

Jack Henry & Associates reported solid Q3 2025 results, with strong recurring revenue growth and expanded margins. While revenue guidance was adjusted due to macroeconomic factors, the company raised its margin and EPS outlook, supported by effective cost controls and growing demand for cloud and payment solutions. Management remains optimistic about long-term growth, driven by robust client migrations, competitive core wins, and innovation in payment technologies.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Wall Street mixed as Alphabet weighs on tech, traders remain cautious ahead of Fed
data.description:
A rebound in U.S. stocks on Wednesday following a two-session losing streak ran out of steam, with Alphabet (GOOG)(GOOGL) weighing on technology stocks. Market participants were also cautious ahead of the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision later in the day. 

The three major averages opened in the green, but were last mixed. The benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) was +0.05% at 5,609.85 points in morning trade, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.54% at 41,048.52 points. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.38% at 17,623.00 points. 

Alphabet (GOOG)(GOOGL) shares sharply turned lower, about -7%, after Apple’s (AAPL) Eddy Cue said the iPhone maker was exploring adding artificial intelligence search to its browser.

Among other active movers, Walt Disney (DIS) surged +10.4%, after the world's second-largest entertainment company announced plans to open its first Middle East theme park in Abu Dhabi and reported a quarterly beat.  

The spotlight was on the Fed. Its monetary policy committee will issue its third interest rate decision of the year at 1400 ET, with traders expecting no change to the federal funds rate. Chair Jerome Powell will head a press conference at 1430 ET, where he will no doubt be peppered by questions about tariffs and the central bank's independence. 

"The Fed is most assuredly expected to keep rates unchanged at Wednesday's meeting as recent data does not support lowering interest rates. The inflation picture remains elevated and sticky and unemployment continues to be relatively contained, so the Fed lacks the necessary ingredients to cut rates," Chris Brigati, chief investment officer at SWBC, said. 

"Wednesday's FOMC meeting is the first one since the tariff situation escalated in early April, and we may receive some clues from Chair Powell at the press conference regarding the potential impact of the trade war on the economy," he said.

"If Powell’s statements lean dovish, he may be suggesting a more accommodative stance and concerns about the employment picture, however, a more hawkish inclination suggests continued restrictiveness and concerns about inflation going forward. Regardless, more clarity and insight will certainly be helpful for the markets to determine the future path forward," Brigati added.

Meanwhile, on the trade front, U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said negotiations with China will begin on Saturday. He was speaking at a House of Representatives Committee on Financial Services hearing.

Turning to the fixed-income markets, U.S. Treasury yields were mixed. The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) was last down nearly 1 basis point to 4.29%, while the shorter-end more rate-sensitive 2-year yield (US2Y) was up 3 basis points to 3.82%. 

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.
